2605.28371.title: From paper to benchmark: agentic, framework-based reproduction of under-specified methods in machine health intelligence
2605.28371.paper_summary: Industrial Prognostics and Health Management (PHM) provides a representative case study for a broader challenge in applied machine learning: translating published papers into executable, benchmark-ready implementations. Reproducing under-specified methods in PHM is particularly difficult due to restricted access to industrial datasets, incomplete reporting of preprocessing and evaluation protocols, and implicit design choices (e.g., windowing, target construction, data splits) that critically affect performance. Existing paper-to-code systems generate implementations for individual papers, but these artifacts are often not directly comparable due to inconsistencies in assumptions and evaluation settings. We introduce \emph{agentic, framework-based PHM paper reproduction}, where an agent translates a paper into a shared PHM benchmark framework via a \emph{slot-binding interface}. This interface maps equations and protocol descriptions into structured components (task definitions, dataset adapters, windowing, targets, models, and evaluators), while explicitly recording unresolved assumptions. The resulting implementations are validated against standardized task contracts and evaluation hooks, enabling consistent and comparable benchmarking. We evaluate this approach on 16 PHM papers, comparing framework-enhanced, skill-based and prompt-based agentic reproduction against a recent framework-free paper-reproduction agent. We assess reproduction success, model-based code evaluation, framework binding of paper assumptions, and cross-paper benchmark comparability under standardized protocols. Our results show that coupling agentic generation with a shared framework transforms paper reproduction from isolated code synthesis into executable, assumption-aware, and systematically comparable benchmark implementations.
2605.28371.paper_summary_zh: 工業預測與健康管理（PHM）提供了一個代表性的案例研究，針對應用機器學習中的更廣泛挑戰：將已發表的論文轉換為可執行的、準備好的基準實現。由於對工業數據集的訪問受限、預處理和評估協議的不完整報告以及影響性能的隱含設計選擇（例如，窗口設置、目標構建、數據拆分），在PHM中重現不明確的方法特別困難。現有的論文到代碼系統為個別論文生成實現，但由於假設和評估設置的不一致，這些產物往往無法直接比較。我們引入了\emph{有主動性的、基於框架的PHM論文重現}，其中一個代理通過\emph{插槽綁定接口}將論文轉換為共享的PHM基準框架。該接口將方程式和協議描述映射到結構化組件（任務定義、數據集適配器、窗口設置、目標、模型和評估器），同時明確記錄未解決的假設。生成的實現將根據標準化的任務合同和評估鉤進行驗證，從而實現一致且可比較的基準測試。我們在16篇PHM論文上評估了這種方法，將框架增強的、基於技能和基於提示的有主動性重現與最近的無框架論文重現代理進行比較。我們評估了重現成功率、基於模型的代碼評估、論文假設的框架綁定以及在標準化協議下的跨論文基準可比性。我們的結果顯示，將有主動性生成與共享框架結合，將論文重現從孤立的代碼合成轉變為可執行的、意識到假設的、系統可比較的基準實現。
2605.28371.author: Raffael Theiler et.al.
2605.28371.authors: Raffael Theiler, Ludovico Comito, David Leko, Leandro Von Krannichfeldt, Lev Telyatnikov, Olga Fink
